title: Hydrochorea marginata
intro: Hydrochorea marginata, also known as Pau ferro, is a plant species native to South America, belonging to the family Sapindaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate pain, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Hydrochorea marginata include a lignan called ferulic acid, as well as other compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hydrochorea marginata include ointments, creams, and oils, which are used topically to treat various conditions, including arthritis, muscle and joint pain, and skin irritations.
